header.title: How Layrix Works
header.intro: Layrix manages your design system across three layers. Here you see how they connect — and which layer to touch when.

flow.title: Elementor Variables
flow.chip: visible in style panel as token

rows.dd_html: Your central source of truth. All design values (colors, spacings, radii, shadows, fonts) and class defaults.
In the Layrix plugin itself — left sidebar → Design Values or Classes.
Global CSS variables that Elementor itself knows about. Layrix values are mirrored here 1:1 — so they are pickable as tokens in the Elementor editor.
Technically in Elementor → Site Settings → Variables — but not recommended. The next Layrix sync would overwrite your values. Better to change them in the Layrix plugin.
Classes like ecf-button, ecf-heading-1, ecf-container-boxed. They reference the variables from layer 2 for their values (padding, font-size, etc.).
In the Elementor class editor — you can add your own properties (background, hover, …). Since 0.6.1.2 Layrix only merges its own properties on sync, your custom values remain.

faqs.a_html: If Auto-Sync is on (default): automatically in the background after every save in Layrix. Otherwise manually via the Sync page or the „Sync now" button on the Status page.

faqs.q: What happens when I change a variable directly in Elementor?
faqs.a_html: The next sync will write the backend value back over your Elementor value. Layer 1 is the source of truth. So always change values in the Layrix plugin.

faqs.q: What if I set custom values on a Layrix class?
faqs.a_html: Your own properties (e.g. background-color, hover state) remain untouched since 0.6.1.2. Layrix only merges its own property keys (padding, font-size, border-radius, …). Earlier versions replaced the entire variants — hence the important fix.

faqs.q: What happens when the plugin is deactivated?
faqs.a_html:
Layer 1 (Layrix settings) stays in the DB. Layer 2 and 3 (Elementor Variables/Classes) also remain in Elementor's database — only the dynamically generated frontend CSS disappears.

Clean exit: Sync page → download CSS → embed into theme. Classes in HTML are then styled even without the plugin.

faqs.q: What if Elementor breaks something (update, another plugin, …)?
faqs.a_html: As long as layer 1 (your Layrix settings) is intact, you can regenerate layer 2 + 3 anytime via sync. Recommendation: export the preset regularly (Sync page → Export preset) as a backup of your settings.

faqs.q: Why do some values survive a sync but others do not?
faqs.a_html: Rule of thumb: Layrix-managed properties (padding, font-size, border-radius, max-width on Layrix classes) win from the backend. Everything else — your own classes, your own variables, custom properties on Layrix classes, per-element styles — remains untouched.
